issues.title: Missing filter on transaction status
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status correctly as per the business rule.

issues.title: Incorrect aggregation
issues.line: SUM(t.amount) as lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders
issues.problem: The query calculates lifetime value and total orders without considering the status of the transaction.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value, COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id END) as total_orders

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic of the query.
issues.fix:
-- Calculate customer lifetime value and total completed orders
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id END) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;

review.corrected_sql:
-- Calculate customer lifetime value and total completed orders
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id END) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query had issues with filtering transaction status and calculating lifetime value correctly. Comments were also added for readability.

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is being calculated incorrectly because failed_count is not defined in the SELECT clause but is used in the calculation.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN to avoid correlated subquery.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which can make it harder to understand for other developers.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the logic of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
WITH failed_transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) as failed_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  WHERE status = 'FAILED'
  GROUP BY transaction_date
),
total_transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) as total_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  GROUP BY transaction_date
)
SELECT t.transaction_date,
       ft.failed_count,
       tt.total_count,
       (ft.failed_count::DECIMAL / tt.total_count) * 100 as failure_rate
FROM total_transactions tt
JOIN failed_transactions ft ON tt.transaction_date = ft.transaction_date
ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the failure rate calculation and a performance issue with a correlated subquery. Readability was also improved by adding comments.

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query is not filtering transactions by 'COMPLETED' status as per the business rule.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining its purpose and logic.
issues.fix:
-- Query to find top spenders by customer tier for dashboard integration
SELECT c.tier, email, SUM(amount) AS total_spend
FROM dim_customer c
JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.tier, email
HAVING total_spend > 5000;

issues.title: Missing join with dim_merchant
issues.problem: The query should join with dim_merchant to get merchant names instead of showing merchant IDs.
issues.fix:
-- Query to find top spenders by customer tier for dashboard integration
SELECT c.tier, c.email, SUM(t.amount) AS total_spend
FROM dim_customer c
JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.tier, c.email
HAVING total_spend > 5000;

review.corrected_sql:
-- Query to find top spenders by customer tier for dashboard integration
SELECT c.tier, c.email, SUM(t.amount) AS total_spend
FROM dim_customer c
JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.tier, c.email
HAVING total_spend > 5000;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the status filter and lacked comments and a join with dim_merchant for readability.

issues.title: Missing filter for completed transactions
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status 'COMPLETED' to calculate revenue.

issues.title: Implicit join syntax
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can lead to less readable and potentially confusing queries.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic.
issues.fix: ADD comments to explain the purpose and logic

review.corrected_sql: SELECT m.merchant_name, SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue, COUNT(*) as txn_count FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01' AND t.status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY m.merchant_name ORDER BY total_revenue LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query has a critical correctness issue by not filtering for completed transactions. It also uses implicit join syntax and lacks comments, which could be improved for readability.
